reasoning: Without access to real-time market data for November 2025, my forecast relies on base rate and structural considerations. Historically, the Federal Reserve leaves its primary policy rate unchanged at roughly 55-60% of all FOMC meetings. However, the March meeting includes the Summary of Economic Projections (SEP), where the Fed is historically somewhat more likely to adjust rates (either to telegraph a pivot or implement a quarterly adjustment). By early 2026, the US economy might either be at a steady neutral rate (favoring "no change" heavily) or still undergoing gradual adjustments (favoring a 25 bps cut). Given this mixture, the probability of "no change" is somewhat lower than the historical unconditional average hold rate but still very plausible. I estimate it at approximately 45%.
